---
title: "Auth0 Setup"
description: "Configure Auth0 for local development."
---

Polydelve delegates all authentication to Auth0. For local dev you need a free Auth0 tenant and a Single Page Application registered inside it.

## 1. Create a dev tenant

1. Sign up or log in at [auth0.com](https://auth0.com).
2. Click your avatar → **Create tenant**. Name it anything (e.g. `polydelve-dev`). Region does not matter for local use.

## 2. Create a SPA application

1. In the dashboard go to **Applications → Applications → Create Application**.
2. Name it (e.g. `Polydelve Local`) and select **Single Page Application**.
3. Click **Create**.

## 3. Configure allowed URLs

On the application's **Settings** tab, set all three fields to `http://localhost:5173` (the Vite dev server port):

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Allowed Callback URLs | `http://localhost:5173` |
| Allowed Logout URLs | `http://localhost:5173` |
| Allowed Web Origins | `http://localhost:5173` |

Scroll down and click **Save Changes**.

## 4. Create an API audience

Auth0 requires an API resource to issue access tokens with an audience claim.

1. Go to **Applications → APIs → Create API**.
2. Name it (e.g. `Polydelve API`) and set the **Identifier** to any URI (e.g. `https://api.polydelve.local`). This value becomes `AUTH0_AUDIENCE`.
3. Click **Create**.

## 5. Copy credentials to `.env`

### Backend

```bash
cp backend/secrets/.env.example backend/secrets/.env
```

Fill in the Auth0 block:

```dotenv
AUTH0_DOMAIN=your-tenant.us.auth0.com
AUTH0_AUDIENCE=https://api.polydelve.local
```

Find `AUTH0_DOMAIN` on the application Settings page (labelled **Domain**).

### Frontend

Check `.env.example` at the repo root for exact variable names. They are likely:

```dotenv
NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH0_DOMAIN=your-tenant.us.auth0.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID=<Client ID from application Settings>
NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH0_AUDIENCE=https://api.polydelve.local
```

`Client ID` is on the same Settings page as the domain.

## 6. Test the login flow

```bash
make dev
```

Open `http://localhost:5173`. Click **Log in** — you should be redirected to the Auth0 Universal Login page and returned to the app after authenticating.

On first successful login the frontend calls `POST /users` to provision your account. You will then see the **UsernameModal** prompting you to pick a username (3–20 characters, letters, numbers, and underscores only). After submitting, the modal will not appear again.

## Troubleshooting

**Redirect loop / `invalid_grant`** — confirm Allowed Callback URLs includes `http://localhost:5173` exactly (no trailing slash).

**401 from the API** — confirm `AUTH0_AUDIENCE` in both `.env` files matches the API Identifier you registered.

**`UsernameModal` appears on every load** — the `POST /users` call is failing; check the backend logs for a 422 or database error.
